rulings.Case Title: PHILLIP GARCIA VS. CARRIE WILSON, IN HER CAPACITCY AS TRUSTEE OF THE et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, July 28, 2014, Line 3: DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: OFF CALENDAR, duplicate entry. (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: PHILLIP GARCIA VS. CARRIE WILSON, IN HER CAPACITCY AS TRUSTEE OF THE et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, July 28, 2014, Line 3: DEFENDANT ANGELO WILSON's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: Sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer to the sixth cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to attach a copy of the contract. Demurrer to the seventh cause of action overruled as Angelo Wilson has no standing to assert res judicata on behalf of another defendant. Moving party and its counsel are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C in future. (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: ARKADY ZELIKOVSKY et al VS. 3520 BALBOA STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction Filed By Plaintiffs
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, July 28, 2014, Line 4: Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction Filed By Plaintiffs: Continued to August 15, 2014. Moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6B and provide courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers to department 501 no later than July 31, 2014 by 4 p.m. with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: EDMUND CHU VS. SHAUNAK SAYTA et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, July 28, 2014, Line 7: DEFENDANT SHAUNAK SAYTA's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges that defendant is a holdover subtenant after the original occupant vacated; that defendant was served with a notice of rent increase; defendant failed to pay any portion of rent; defendant was served with a notice to cure or quit; and defendant failed to comply with the notice. Any factual arguments regarding composition of the unit and physical possession of different portions of the unit are improperly raised on the demurrer. (501/AJR)
